title: राम मङ्गलाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राम मङ्गलाष्टकम् ॥

मङ्गलं कोसलेन्द्राय महनीयगुणात्मने।
चक्रवर्तिकुलोद्भूताय सार्वभौमाय मङ्गलम्॥१॥

वेदवेदान्तवेद्याय मेघश्यामलमूर्तये।
पुंसां मोहनरूपाय पुण्यश्लोकाय मङ्गलम्॥२॥

विश्वामित्रप्रियाय च मखसंरक्षणाय च।
ताटकान्तकरामाय मारीचान्तकृते नमः मङ्गलम्॥३॥

श्रीशिवचापभञ्जनाय मिथिलापुरवासिने।
सीतालक्ष्मणसंयुक्ताय जानकीशाय मङ्गलम्॥४॥

पितृवाक्यप्रतिपालाय रघुवंशविवर्धिने।
चित्रकूटनिवासाय दण्डकारण्यवासिने मङ्गलम्॥५॥

मारीचान्तकराय च वालिसंहारकारिणे।
सुग्रीवप्रतिपालाय सेतुबन्धाय मङ्गलम्॥६॥

रावणान्तकराय च सीतोद्धारणहेतवे।
पुष्पकारोहणाय च पट्टाभिषिक्ताय मङ्गलम्॥७॥

रामराज्यप्रदात्रे च सर्वधर्मस्वरूपिणे।
रामायणप्रणेतृभ्यो रामभद्राय मङ्गलम्॥८॥

मङ्गलाष्टकमेतद्यो रामस्य प्रपठेन्नरः।
सर्वदा सर्वकार्येषु सिद्धिर्भवति निश्चिता॥

॥ इति श्री राम मङ्गलाष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Rama Mangalashtakam ||

Mangalam Koshalendraya Mahaneeyagunatmane.
Chakravarti-kulodbhutaya Sarvabhaumaya Mangalam.||1||

Veda-vedanta-vedyaya Meghashyamala-moortaye.
Pumsam Mohana-roopaya Punyashlokaya Mangalam.||2||

Vishvamitra-priyaya Cha Makha-samrakshanaya Cha.
Tatakantaka-Ramaya Marichaantakrite Namah Mangalam.||3||

Shri Shiva-chapa-bhanjanaya Mithilapura-vasine.
Sita-Lakshmana-samyuktaya Janakishaya Mangalam.||4||

Pitru-vakya-pratipalaya Raghuvansha-vivardhine.
Chitrakuta-nivasaya Dandakaranya-vasine Mangalam.||5||

Marichantakaraya Cha Vali-samhara-karine.
Sugriva-pratipalaya Setubandhaya Mangalam.||6||

Ravanantakaraya Cha Sitoddharana-hetave.
Pushpakarohanaya Cha Pattabhishiktaya Mangalam.||7||

Ramarajya-pradatre Cha Sarva-dharma-svaroopine.
Ramayana-pranetribhyo Ramabhadraya Mangalam.||8||

Mangalashtakam-Etadyo Ramasya Prapathennnarah.
Sarvada Sarva-karyeshu Siddhir-bhavati Nishchita.

|| Iti Shri Rama Mangalashtakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. कोशलनरेश, महान गुणों वाले, चक्रवर्ती वंश में उत्पन्न, सार्वभौम राम को मंगल।

२. वेद-वेदान्त द्वारा ज्ञेय, मेघश्याम, मनोहर रूप वाले, पुण्यश्लोक राम को मंगल।

३. विश्वामित्र के प्रिय, यज्ञ-रक्षक, ताटकावधकर्ता, मारीचनाशक राम को मंगल।

४. शिवधनुष भंजन करने वाले, मिथिलापुरवासी, सीता-लक्ष्मण सहित जानकीनाथ राम को मंगल।

५. पितृवचन का पालन करने वाले, रघुवंशवर्धक, चित्रकूटवासी, दण्डकारण्यवासी राम को मंगल।

६. मारीचवध, वालिसंहार, सुग्रीव-प्रतिपालक, सेतुबन्धक राम को मंगल।

७. रावणवधकर्ता, सीतोद्धारक, पुष्पकविमान आरोही, राज्याभिषिक्त राम को मंगल।

८. रामराज्य देने वाले, सर्वधर्मस्वरूप, रामायण के प्रणेता रामभद्र को मंगल।

जो इस राम मंगलाष्टक का पाठ करता है, उसे सर्वदा सब कार्यों में निश्चित सिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Invokes the auspicious blessings of Lord Rama through his entire life story
Traditionally recited to invoke success in all endeavors with certainty (nishchit siddhi)
Covers Rama's journey from birth to Rama Rajya in eight verses
Ideal for Ram Navami and beginning new ventures
Fills the devotee's life with dharma and righteousness
Powerful protection prayer for the entire family
description: Dedicated to Lord Rama, the Rama Mangalashtakam (राम मङ्गलाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Valmiki Ramayana tradition. Devotees recite this prayer to cover Rama's journey from birth to Rama Rajya in eight verses and invoke the auspicious blessings of Lord Rama through his entire life story. It is also said to fill the devotee's life with dharma and righteousness. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ram Navami and Dussehra. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Rama Mangalashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Rama Mangalashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight auspicious verses covering Lord Rama's divine journey for blessings and success.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Rama Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rama Mangalashtakam (श्री राम मङ्गलाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse Sanskrit composition that pronounces mangalam (auspiciousness) upon Lord Rama by tracing his entire avatara-lila in eight successive verses — from his birth as Koshalendra (king of Koshala) and Chakravarti-kuloodbhuta (born in the Chakravartin lineage) in verse 1, through the Vishvamitra yajna-rakshana, Tataka-vadha, Mareecha-pratighata, Shiva-dhanu-bhanga at Mithila, Sita-vivaha, vana-vasa with Sita-Lakshmana, the Chitrakuta and Dandakaranya years, the Vali-vadha and Sugriva-pratipala, the Setu-bandha at Rameshwaram, the Ravana-vadha and Sita-uddhara, the Pushpaka return and final Pattabhisheka at Ayodhya — culminating in Rama-rajya. The closing phalashruti promises nishchit siddhi (assured success in all undertakings) to the devotee who recites it. (Source: Valmiki Ramayana stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does each verse of the Rama Mangalashtakam invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The eight mangalam-verses move chronologically through Rama's life: (1) Mangalam to the Koshalendra, sarvabhauma chakravarti-kula; (2) Mangalam to the Vedavedanta-vedya, megha-shyama, punyashloka Rama; (3) Mangalam to the Vishvamitra-priya, Tataka-Mareecha-antaka; (4) Mangalam to the Shiva-chapa-bhanjana, Mithila-vasi, Janaki-natha; (5) Mangalam to the pitru-vakya-pratipala, Chitrakuta-Dandakaranya-vasi; (6) Mangalam to the Mareecha-Vali-samhara-kari, Sugriva-pratipala, Setu-bandha; (7) Mangalam to the Ravana-antaka, Sita-uddharana, Pushpaka-arohana, Pattabhishikta; (8) Mangalam to the Rama-rajya-pradata, sarva-dharma-svarupa, Ramayana-pranetru-Rama-bhadra. Together the eight verses are a complete poetic synopsis of the Ramayana itself. (Source: Rama Mangalashtakam text; Valmiki Ramayana parallels.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Rama Mangalashtakam recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rama Mangalashtakam is most auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to Lord Rama and the Guru), and especially on Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami — Rama's appearance day), Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami — the celestial wedding day of Sita-Rama at Janakpur), Hanuman Jayanti, Dussehra / Vijayadashami (commemorating Rama's victory over Ravana), Diwali (commemorating Rama's return to Ayodhya and Pattabhisheka), Akshaya Tritiya, and during the Adhik Maas Rama-katha pravachanas. Devotees also recite it before beginning any new venture, journey, business, marriage, or auspicious household event because the phalashruti specifically promises sarva-karyeshu sarvada siddhir-bhavati nishchita — assured success in all undertakings at all times. (Sources: Drikpanchang Rama festival calendar; traditional mangalashtakam recitation custom.)

seoFaqs.question: What is a mangalashtakam and how is it different from a stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: A mangalashtakam is a specific genre of devotional Sanskrit composition in which each verse ends with the word mangalam (auspiciousness) — pronouncing or invoking blessing upon a particular deity. Mangala-stotras are traditionally recited at the conclusion of pujas, parayanas, and Sahasranama-recitations (as mangalam phala-shruti), at the start of new ventures, before marriage muhurats, and during important household sankalpas. While a regular stotra (e.g. Rama Stotram, Rama Chalisa) is a hymn of praise that may invoke many feelings — devotion, surrender, petition, meditation — a mangalashtakam exclusively pronounces auspiciousness as a benediction. Other famous mangalashtakams include Krishna Mangalashtakam, Shiva Mangalashtakam, Subramanya Mangalashtakam, and Lakshmi Mangalashtakam. (Source: classical Sanskrit stotra-shastra.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the phalashruti of Rama Mangalashtakam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phala-shruti verse declares: Mangalashtakam-etadyo Ramasya prapathennarah, Sarvada sarvakaryeshu siddhir-bhavati nishchita — the man who recites this Mangalashtakam of Rama daily attains assured success (siddhi nishchita) in all undertakings (sarva-karyeshu) at all times (sarvada). Tradition particularly attributes the following blessings: (1) sarva-karya-siddhi — fulfilment of all dharmic objectives, (2) mangala-arabhana — auspicious commencement of new ventures, journeys, businesses and marriages, (3) vighna-nashana — removal of obstacles, (4) Rama-kripa — direct grace of Lord Rama, (5) kula-vriddhi — prosperity and continuity of the family lineage, and (6) dharma-rakshana — protection of dharmic life. It is one of the most concise yet comprehensive Rama-prayers in the entire Sanskrit corpus. (Source: Rama Mangalashtakam phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Rama Mangalashtakam often chanted at weddings and new ventures?
seoFaqs.answer: Rama Mangalashtakam is traditionally chanted at Hindu weddings (especially during the mangalashtakam segment when the priest pronounces blessings on the couple just before Kanyadaan or Saptapadi), at Griha Pravesh (housewarming), Bhumi Pujan, Annaprashan, Vidyarambha, business openings, Vahan Puja, journey commencements, and at the conclusion of any major puja or Ramayana parayana. The reason is two-fold: theologically, Rama is the Maryada Purushottama — the perfect embodiment of dharma whose blessing assures right-conduct and right-outcome; and textually, the phalashruti explicitly promises nishchit siddhi (assured success) sarvada sarva-karyeshu (always in all undertakings). The compact eight-verse length also makes it ritually convenient to recite during muhurat-bound ceremonies. (Sources: Hindu marriage paddhati; traditional mangalashtakam recitation custom.)
